perturbation.original_text: Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization. Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refers to data and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient, regardless of whether it is clearly labeled or designated as "Confidential Information." Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be made publicly available are excluded, except for information disclosed by either party in violation of this agreement and without authorization. Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is changed to include all relevant business and technical information disclosed, regardless of labeling. This contradicts later sections that refer to 'Confidential Information' as only that which is 'clearly labeled', creating uncertainty. Specifically, section 2 refers to copies of the confidential information disclosed by the other party, whether intentionally or not, unless the disclosure is allowed by a written consent singed by the other party, now with this new definition of confidential information, it is not clear whether that means anything disclosed or only that labeled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties shall strictly limit the access to the confidential information to their responsible representati ves only for the purpose specified hereunder. Neither party shall provide a third party with copies or duplicates of the confidential information disclosed by the other party or its representative, whether intentionally or not, unless the disclosure is allowed by a written consent singed by the other party. In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as data and information disclosed with a clear label or designation. The changed text broadens this definition to include all relevant business and technical information disclosed, regardless of labeling. The contradicted text refers to 'copies or duplicates of the confidential information disclosed by the other party', implying that such information is identifiable and distinct. The modified definition makes it unclear what constitutes 'confidential information' for the purpose of the contradicted section, as it removes the requirement for a clear label. This creates a contradiction, as it's no longer clear if the clause about copies applies to everything disclosed or only labeled information. The Perturbation Explanation provided, further emphasizes this conflict, solidifying that a strong contradiction has been introduced.

perturbation.original_text: The following items are not covered by the concept of this Confidential Information: (i) Information in the public domain at the effective date of the Confidentiality Agreement or information which becomes part of the public domain other than through non- compliance with the Confidentiality Agreement. (ii) Informatio n that was already in the Recipient's possession before the Recipient received it from Entrusting party. (iii) Information that the Recipient has obtained from a third party without any obligation to keep the information secret. (iv) Information that the Recipient ha s obtained by unlawful means from and out of the domain of either parties beyond despite the due care and diligence exercised by either parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The following items are not covered by the concept of this Confidential Information: (i) Information in the public domain at the effective date of the Confidentiality Agreement or information which becomes part of the public domain through any means. (ii) Information that was already in the Recipient's possession before the Recipient received it from Entrusting party. (iii) Information that the Recipient has obtained from a third party without any obligation to keep the information secret. (iv) Information that the Recipient ha s obtained by unlawful means from and out of the domain of either parties beyond despite the due care and diligence exercised by either parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than through non- compliance with the Confidentiality Agreement' in the definition exception of 'Confidential Information', it creates a contradiction with section 4.1 which requires the agreement to be interpreted in accordance to the laws of the People's Republic of China. If Confidential Information becomes public due to a breach of the contract, this section now protects the violating party, contradicting the intent of confidentiality and thus creating uncertainty on if a party is reliable for a penalty for a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing the clause 'other than through non-compliance with the Confidentiality Agreement.' This creates a scenario where information becoming public due to a breach of the confidentiality agreement is still considered an exception to Confidential Information, thus protecting the breaching party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the agreement is governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China and accepts the jurisdiction of the people's courts for any disputes. The change undermines the enforceability of the agreement under Chinese law regarding confidentiality breaches, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties shall strictly limit the access to the confidential information to their responsible representati ves only for the purpose specified hereunder. Neither party shall provide a third party with copies or duplicates of the confidential information disclosed by the other party or its representative, whether intentionally or not, unless the disclosure is allowed by a written consent singed by the other party. In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties shall make reasonable efforts to limit the access to the confidential information to their responsible representatives only for the purpose specified hereunder. Neither party shall provide a third party with copies or duplicates of the confidential information disclosed by the other party or its representative, whether intentionally or not, unless the disclosure is allowed by a written consent singed by the other party. In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall strictly limit' to 'shall make reasonable efforts to limit' weakens the obligation to protect confidential information. This creates ambiguity when considering section 4.1, which states that the agreement should be interpreted in accordance with the law and any disputes should be settled in Chinese courts. The original strict obligation would likely be interpreted as a legally enforceable duty, while the modified 'reasonable efforts' standard introduces subjectivity, potentially leading to conflicting interpretations in court. It is not clear when a party had done enough to protect that data, and can lead to potential conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The original text 'shall strictly limit' imposed a firm obligation to protect confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'shall make reasonable efforts to limit'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which mandates that the agreement be interpreted in accordance with Chinese law, implying a definite and enforceable obligation. The 'reasonable efforts' standard introduces subjectivity that could lead to disputes and conflicting interpretations, as it is unclear what constitutes 'reasonable efforts' under Chinese law. Thus, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction, fitting the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because it makes interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives shall be construed to constitute a temporary license to use the other party's intellectual property for the sole purpose of the cooperative project, but not an assignment. nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be construed to constitute an assignment' to 'shall be construed to constitute a temporary license' contradicts the original intention of not granting any intellectual property rights. Now if there is a temporary license it is not clear who owns what, and where the liabilities are if the temporary license does infringes on a 3rd party. This creates a conflict with the overall understanding of intellectual property handling established in section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.  Intellectual Property Rights 3.1 Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'temporary license' where the original text and the contradicted text explicitly state that there is no assignment or grant of rights. This creates a clear contradiction as a temporary license implies a grant of rights, conflicting with the original intent. The explanation highlights the uncertainty and potential liabilities introduced by the temporary license, further emphasizing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall remain effective for two years , and shall come into force as from the date when both parties sign and stamp the company chop on the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall remain effective for two years, unless terminated earlier as per section 6, and shall come into force as from the date when both parties sign and stamp the company chop on the agreement. After the 2 years, it will renew automatically for every year.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'After the 2 years, it will renew automatically for every year' to section 5.1 directly contradicts the termination clause in section 6.1(i) 'by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement;' If the agreement renews automatically it is no longer terminated automatically after two years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause to the agreement's duration ('After the 2 years, it will renew automatically for every year'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, specifically section i) which states the agreement shall be terminated 'by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement'. The automatic renewal means the agreement doesn't terminate automatically after two years, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Should the agreement be terminated, for points (ii) to (iv) above, either party s hall intimate the other party of such termination in writing duly addressed to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Should the agreement be terminated, for points (ii) to (iv) above, either party may intimate the other party of such termination in writing duly addressed to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall intimate' to 'may intimate' in the termination notification clause removes the mandatory requirement for notification upon termination (other than by time lapse). This creates a contradiction with the expectation set in the governing law section (4.1) where all disputes shall be governed in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China. This could cause an interpretation issue if the notification is deemed essential for a valid termination, impacting the overall rights and obligations laid out in the termination procedure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'shall intimate', creating a mandatory obligation to notify the other party of termination. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', making notification optional. The contradicted text specifies that the agreement 'shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China'. If Chinese law requires notification for a valid termination, the 'may' clause creates a contradiction because it allows for termination without notification, potentially violating governing law. The change introduces an ambiguity and potential inconsistency between the termination procedure and the governing law, fitting the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
